approach: Daubechies scaling-function basis + Shor SDP relaxation of inf_c max_t c^T M(t) c
verdict_short: Daubechies wavelet basis is mathematically the WRONG direction for an LB on C_{1a}: restricting f to a finite-dim wavelet subspace gives val_W >= C_{1a} (an UPPER bound, smaller subspace = larger inf), and Shor relaxation of val_W gives only a LB on val_W itself, not on C_{1a}.  No analog of CS Lemma 1 (windowed test-value inequality) exists for wavelet coefficients that would connect the LB to C_{1a} universally.
verdict_long:
The probe set up the autoconvolution (f*f)(t) = c^T M(t) c in the Daubechies db_N scaling-function basis at level J, with M(t) Hankel from the autoconvolution Psi(y) = (phi*phi)(y).  Three configurations were tested: (N=4,J=5,K=10), (N=4,J=6,K=26), (N=8,J=6,K=18). For each: primal min-max QP (SLSQP from random starts) gave an UPPER BOUND on val_W := inf_{c valid} max_t c^T M(t) c, and an order-1 Shor SDP with RLT cuts gave a LOWER BOUND on val_W.  Best UB = 5.4197, best LB = 2.1540334674962405.

CRITICAL MATHEMATICAL OBSERVATION: val_W is the inf over the wavelet subspace, so val_W(N, J) >= C_{1a}.  Hence any LB on val_W is NOT a LB on C_{1a}.  For the bin-mass Lasserre track in lasserre/, the analogous quantity val(d) is rescued by the CS windowed inequality: ||f*f||_inf >= mu^T M_W mu for any window W, where mu are bin masses of ANY nonneg f -- this gives val(d) <= C_{1a}.  No such universal inequality is available for Daubechies wavelet coefficients because the projection of f onto a wavelet subspace can have arbitrarily different autoconvolution from f itself (when the high-frequency tail g_J = f - f_J is nontrivial).

DEAD-END.  The wavelet basis is suitable for UB construction (find a smooth f with low max-autocorrelation), not for LB certification.  The current monomial Lasserre track is mathematically the right framework for LB.

experiments.note: val_W is on subspace; LB does NOT prove anything about C_{1a}.

extra_metrics.critical_note: val_W is on the WAVELET SUBSPACE only. val_W(N,J) >= C_{1a} (UB direction). Shor LB on val_W is NOT a LB on C_{1a}. No analog of CS Lemma 1 windowed inequality exists for wavelet coefficients, so this approach cannot produce a universal LB on C_{1a}.
